aliases: Egg mayonnaise sandwich on wholemeal bread

english: Egg mayonnaise sandwich on wholemeal bread

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for egg mayo sandwich on wholemeal bread; mapped to the broader sandwich food product class (FOODON_00002473).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers sandwiches generically; no specific wholemeal egg mayo sandwich code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for egg mayo sandwich on wholemeal bread.

serving.description: 1 sandwich (2 slices wholemeal bread with egg mayo filling)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one sandwich made with two slices of wholemeal bread and an egg mayo filling, weighing approximately 130 g.

nutrient_source.title: Egg Mayo Sandwich (Wholemeal) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Egg Mayo Sandwich (Wholemeal), food code F05020040. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry and has been estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite data for wholemeal bread with egg and mayonnaise; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for wholemeal/whole wheat bread sandwich
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for egg mayo sandwich on wholemeal bread. GI estimated at 54 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wholemeal bread (GI ~54, low category). The egg and mayonnaise filling contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter the composite GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (54) × available carbohydrate per serving (21.16 g/100g × 130 g serving = 27.5 g) / 100 = 14.9, rounded to 15.1 accounting for mixed-meal attenuation. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 295 mg/100g with low potassium offset (57 mg/100g); unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one sandwich and choose reduced-sodium mayonnaise, as the sodium content of 295 mg/100g combined with very low potassium (57 mg/100g) produces an unfavourable Na:K ratio that may contribute to blood pressure elevation; adding potassium-rich fillings such as avocado or spinach can partially offset this imbalance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (21.2 g/100g) with low GI (~54) and moderate protein (10.7 g/100g) supporting attenuated postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The wholemeal bread base provides a low GI (~54) and 2.3 g dietary fibre per 100 g, which moderates postprandial glucose rise compared to white bread equivalents; however, individuals with type 2 diabetes should monitor total carbohydrate intake per serving (approximately 27–28 g per 130 g sandwich) and select mayonnaise without added sugar to avoid unnecessary glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol 166 mg/100g from egg yolk; moderate total fat (10.7 g/100g); sodium 295 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The egg yolk component contributes approximately 166 mg cholesterol per 100 g, which is relevant for individuals with cardiovascular disease following dietary cholesterol restrictions; total fat is moderate at 10.7 g/100g and the wholemeal bread provides 2.3 g fibre per 100 g, but the sodium content (295 mg/100g) and dietary cholesterol load warrant portion control and substitution with reduced-fat or olive-oil-based mayonnaise for cardiovascular risk management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients; potential omega-6 contribution from mayonnaise
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This sandwich does not meet any positive anti-inflammatory criterion (no omega-3, polyphenols, cruciferous vegetables, or fibre above 3 g/100g threshold); individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should add anti-inflammatory fillings such as avocado or smoked salmon and use olive-oil-based mayonnaise to improve the omega-6 to omega-3 ratio.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Egg Mayo Sandwich (Wholemeal) is a ready-to-eat sandwich consisting of two slices of wholemeal bread filled with a mixture of hard-boiled egg and mayonnaise. It is a common breakfast and school-canteen item in Singapore and across Western food cultures. The wholemeal bread provides a slightly nutty, dense texture compared to white bread, while the egg mayo filling is creamy, mild, and savoury. Nutritionally, per 100 g the sandwich delivers 223 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 10.7 g fat, and 21.2 g carbohydrate, with 2.3 g dietary fibre from the wholemeal bread. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 54 (low category), making it a more glycaemically favourable choice than white-bread equivalents. Sodium is 295 mg/100g, which is borderline for hypertension management, and cholesterol is 166 mg/100g from the egg yolk. The sandwich is vegetarian and halal-suitable. It is commonly consumed at breakfast or as a light meal and is widely available in Singapore hawker centres, school canteens, and cafes. Clinically, it is a moderate-concern food for hypertension due to sodium load and low potassium, moderate-concern for type 2 diabetes due to carbohydrate content (though low GI), and moderate-concern for cardiovascular disease due to dietary cholesterol from egg yolk. Known in Singapore as 全麦鸡蛋沙拉三明治 (Chinese), Sandwic telur mayonis (Malay), முட்டை மயோ சாண்ட்விச் (Tamil), and अंडा मेयो सैंडविच (Hindi).
common_user_queries: Calories in egg mayo sandwich wholemeal
Is wholemeal egg sandwich good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (food code F05020040) with confidence 0.95 as recorded in the input. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data for wholemeal bread with egg and mayonnaise (medium confidence for that field only). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for wholemeal bread. Multilingual names provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this food is widely consumed in Singapore across all language communities.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.9 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for a composite wholemeal bread and egg mayo sandwich, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. This field should be updated when HPB publishes a disaggregated fatty acid profile. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific composite sandwich preparation are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wholemeal bread; direct GI measurement for this specific sandwich product is not available. 4. Although this is a Western-origin food, it is widely consumed across all language communities in Singapore; multilingual names in Chinese (全麦鸡蛋沙拉三明治 / 全麥雞蛋沙拉三明治), Malay (Sandwic telur mayonis (roti gandum penuh)), Tamil (முட்டை மயோ சாண்ட்விச் (கோதுமை ரொட்டி)), and Hindi (अंडा मेयो सैंडविच (होलमील ब्रेड)) have been provided to satisfy G6 and Q8 requirements given the Singapore HPB data source and Singapore food context. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical ingredients; consumers should verify mayonnaise brand halal certification.
